title: × anterioherorchis olida
constituents: × anterioherorchis olida has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and asthma, allowing people to engage in daily activities with reduced discomfort. Additionally, this plant contains coumarins, like scopoletin and umbelliferone, which exhibit anticoagulant properties, helping to prevent blood clots and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The presence of phenolic acids, including caffeic and ferulic acid, in × anterioherorchis olida also contributes to its medicinal value, as these compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that aid in protecting against infections and promoting overall well-being. Furthermore, the plant's content of terpenoids, such as limonene and pinene, has been found to have anti-cancer properties and to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
